title: It's Not Supposed to Be This Way

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Minds are great at painting stories of how life should be. They whisper that you are supposed to be happy almost every day, and the slight waves of sadness should happen only to remind you what happiness feels like. You prefer your loved ones to be in a good mood and for problems never to occur. Most of all, you would be happy to remove the words “uncertain,” “brutal,” and “disappointing” from your vocabulary.

You live with certain expectations in an uncertain world. It’s human to crave the best for yourself and become attached to the outcome. But nearly eight billion people and billions of other things on this planet, along with you, leave little space for control. People live in connection with the world around them, which implies that hundreds of things you don’t have power over went right for you and allowed you to enjoy your breakfast today.

Sometimes you want to grab the wheel to gain control of your destination when suddenly the weather changes, it gets dark, and you have to make unplanned stops and sharp turns. Everyone is familiar with the feeling of opening your palms to catch and ending up with bruises. It’s not always as it’s supposed to be. But don’t give all your attention to the outcome that wasn’t a part of your plan.

You can make disappointment a dark place or a place where you can grow. It depends on your actions after experiencing these feelings.

Stay tuned to find your guidance through disappointments. Lysa TerKeurst encourages thinking of all the great things God has planted around you instead of focusing on the bad. Spend time appreciating the good, as it’s always there — it just needs to be seen.

chapters.title: Think why this situation is right for you
chapters.text:
People wish to live their best lives and strive for excellence. Lysa TerKeurst explains it as the human heart formed in Eden, where everything was ideal. So your expectations come from the memory that achieving perfection is possible. Expecting total perfection will transform every outcome into failure because nothing can be perfect.

People Photoshop their photos, put on a happy face at a party they’re not enjoying, or get offended when others don’t fulfill their expectations. They tend to compare their lives with made-up images their mind creates, and then come the feelings of disappointment and not being enough. Don’t fear disappointment, and don’t bury it inside you. It exists so you can reflect upon what to do differently to live up to your hopes.

Your feelings should have a voice — let them happen and guide you.

But your emotions shouldn’t be your only voice. Pain and unfulfilled expectations are God’s way of leading you through the lesson you must learn to fulfill your dreams.

Different situations are not a burden; they are happening for you. Be attentive to what you can learn from your pain. Focus on gaining something from a disappointment rather than how much it hurts. You can start appreciating life more and find out what you want through it.

As Lysa was going through a divorce, her world fell apart. It was not only the funeral of her twenty-five-year-long relationship but of the life that she was used to. She felt so much pain — as if everything broke not just to pieces but to dust. And there is no going back from that. However, as heartbreaking as it is, dust is God’s favorite component to create something new.

When Lysa realized her husband was having an affair, her grief filled the space around her; she needed the planet to stop. At these devastating times, Lysa thought she could do better than God. But God knows what’s best for you and intends to keep you connected to yourself and Himself.

As the moon has its phases of the beginning, culmination, and ending, so does everything in life. People, your social status, career path, or well-being constantly shift. And the most tragic end can create the space for a better beginning.

chapters.title: Trust the process
chapters.text:
Can you imagine yourself saying, “this is a great moment to break into pieces to grow”? Probably not, so don’t blame God when He sends you unexpected hardships. He helps you build what’s best rather than stay comfortable or settle for less. That’s an intelligent and rational way to put it, right? But what should you do when every cell is hurting from pain, and you want to quit coping with this problem like one quits their job?

No words or exercises can immediately fix the heartbreak or disappointment — like there is no pill to heal a broken leg quickly. Healing from trauma and growing from it is a process.

The only way out of a dark tunnel is through. That’s why the first step to healing pain is feeling it.

Lysa ended up in the critical care unit of her local hospital, and her health collapsed. She felt pain so sharply she could sense the passage of seconds during her day. Lysa begged God to stop the pain, and He didn’t show up. But isn’t this what God is for — to do something when you desperately need him? And where is God when wars and genocides happen? Where is He when a little kid dies from cancer?

It turned out that the pain saved Lysa’s life. It made doctors take more tests, perform the surgery, and continue the treatment. Lysa’s hurt was a trigger to make multiple efforts to cure her. And the miracle happened — she lived. Your physical or emotional suffering is a signal for a more significant change. It may get so intense that you start losing your trust in God. But God wants to help; He doesn’t want to hurt you or for you to ignore the problem. He intends to save and teach you through suffering. Your part is to see it. Trust God; He has a broader view of your life.

Things may not make sense when you are full of emotions or in the middle of your path. Don’t ask for knowledge that you might not be able to handle. Try to see the purpose through suffering. Stop drowning in the disappointment of unmet expectations; you can lose a reason for all of it. None of it is easy, but it is how one survives hardships — trusting God, embracing the feelings, and seeing the lessons.

chapters.title: Be the artist of your life
chapters.text:
When you feel the presence of pain more than the walls of your house, you pray for all existing problems to disappear. You can’t just step out of being hurt or having to deal with challenges, but you definitely can quit a few things. That would create space for healing and listening to God’s voice.

Lysa stopped using television and social media. She focused on receiving rather than giving away, and it helped her heal. Focus on keeping your light and warmth inside; don’t burn for others. Before doing something, ask yourself if it serves you in your recovery process, and then decide whether it’s worth it.

Slow pace and patience are necessary steps to healing.

You are often confident when you feel strong, but being exposed and embracing your inner world isn’t as enjoyable because not everyone can create a safe space and be kind and accepting. The judgments of others won’t have power over you if you have control over your feelings. Don’t let the statements of others evoke your insecurities. If someone says, “I don’t want to be with you,“ don’t translate it as “I’m not enough.” Or if your parents don’t accept your feelings, it doesn’t mean you are wrong to have them.

Don’t be scared of who you are. Instead, invite your scariest thoughts and insecurities inside your mind. Expose yourself to them and try to understand them. This way, you will decide who you are alone, and others’ opinions won’t leave you shattered. You are beautiful, unique, loved, and enough as you are. Don’t believe the hurtful words unhappy people say.

When life doesn’t go as planned, you find yourself in complete darkness and silence instead of a room full of light, warmth, and laughter. You lose the picture of the future you vividly painted in your head. A crash of something turns into a blank canvas in the book of your life, and it’s up to you to fill it in. Don’t let a voice of not being good enough get in the way of your artistic creation.

God wants you to feel loved and free to create. And Satan, your enemy, wants you to feel stuck in pain. Drowning in disappointment and imperfections makes you settle for a job you don’t like or relationships that are not fulfilling. As a result, you may throw the paintbrush away and tolerate mediocre and hurtful things you don’t deserve.

chapters.title: Imperfections help you improve your life
chapters.text:
Humans are imperfect and unfinished; therefore, their destiny is to create. Accepting your imperfections makes you more compassionate to others. You can build meaningful and deep connections by saying, “I see you as you are, and I’m here with you through this.”

Don’t let the illusion of perfection stop you from being who you are and creating who you want to be.

If only everyone knew how beautiful they are when they are real. That’s what attracts people to art — the feeling of authenticity. And that’s how we end up loving someone — through genuinely seeing them. Seeing a human being trying to express themselves through creation is fascinating — “Look! That’s me. I am my flaws, strengths, scars, and merits. And I am free.” You are here to feel all the colors of life. Don’t be afraid to travel through the spectrum of all these feelings.

I don’t want to let disappointment and heartbreak cause me to approach this life more cautious than creative. More critical than compassionate. More cynical than surrendered. ~ Lysa TerKeurst

Dealing with difficult times is like walking a tightrope — always balancing between falling and moving on. There is danger, pain, and darkness in the air. But this road exists so that you can be ready to fulfill your purpose or find within yourself what it takes to achieve your dreams. For example, having a happy and loving family requires loving yourself first, which is difficult to achieve.

Sometimes, going through a lesson is a must to handle the happiness ahead of you. And even when it feels like more than you can take, remember that God sends you hardships as essential ingredients for success and for making your life more beautiful. Feeling weak or like you don’t have enough faith is expected when you get overwhelmed with troubles. But don’t drown in these emotions; use them as a signal for reflection and change.

Moreover, stay conscious about your desires — they can come from the enemy trying to isolate you from God’s truth and ruin you. If you made a mistake or are about to make one, shed a light of reflection on it, and see God’s love and wisdom there.

Did you know? A big emotional wave of crying, screaming, or freaking out signals an unresolved wound.

chapters.title: You are in charge of your perception
chapters.text:
You may feel awkward because life is awkward. It is also confusing, messy, and unpredictable. Its turns can catch you off guard. Understandably, you probably crave a sense of stability and signs that you will be just fine, but life can make it hard to be confident — that’s how you might lose your resolve.

Sometimes you must learn to swim and catch your breath in “I don’t know” moments, “If you love me, why would you pull away,” “I’m not in the right place,” or “I don’t see any sense in it, but I have no choice for now” cases. Perceive these moments as your navigators and as integral elements of who you are supposed to be. Your “I don’t know” is normal and very human. It might steer you away from the knowledge that is a burden for now.

Every pain has its purpose; be eager to see it.

Stay close to God, and your heart will fill with peace and comfort. You are the author of your life. Someone can spill the ink on your canvas or try to strike through your most essential parts. But your eyes and your mind control the perception. Ensure it has love, trust in God’s purpose, compassion, and patience.

Misunderstanding your life path means the wrong interpretation of God’s will. What if your pain and disappointments are the doors to your soul you’d never want to be without? What if they lead you to your best self? God allows suffering, but He wants you to transform it to your benefit and promises to be with you along the way. Notice all the different ways He can manifest Himself to you.

Finding purpose in suffering is often challenging. It doesn’t mean you are wrong to feel pain and struggle. It is impossible to say, “don't worry, be happy” all the time. Let yourself feel the hurt going through your veins and darkness filling your world, but don’t get stuck there. You have the power to change it when the time comes. And you can find at least small glimpses of happiness and purpose in your hardships.

To live is to love. To love is to risk pain. To risk pain is to live. It’s what it means to truly be human. ~ Lysa TerKeurst

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
It’s hard to be human; you are a complex creature with so much going on inside and outside. However, disappointments and suffering are there to shape your character. They help you become what you want to be. It is the path you must go through to fulfill your calling.

Your wounds are prompts to act. It is God’s way of showing you what you must learn to be happy and stay connected to yourself and Him. Practice compassion for yourself and others, making people feel less hurt and alone.

Escaping hardships will rob you of life’s gifts, so face them bravely. The efforts you make help you receive what’s best for you. It’s like exercising every day — one step at a time, an effort, or a challenge will lead you to the desired result.

Fill your heart with trust because God sees and knows the bigger picture. Use all your strength to move forward, as you are probably just beginning your journey. At the start, it is hard to see the meaning of it all. But be patient and let God lead you where you are supposed to end up. Be the artist of your blank canvas — create! Turn the dust of your heartbreak into clay. Be the potter eager to build. And be with love, trust, and connection to God.

Try this

Write a reflection answering these questions:

• What is there to learn from your hardship?
• What benefits can you gain from the place you are at?
• What are the small glimpses of joy in your struggles?
